title: Pavonia tuberculata
intro: Pavonia tuberculata is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, known for its vibrant pink or purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pavonia tuberculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Pavonia tuberculata is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Pavonia tuberculata is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. The Malvaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as mallows. Pavonia tuberculata is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the genus Pavonia, which is a group of plants that are characterized by their showy flowers and hairy leaves. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.
